name: Winslow Santa Fe
description_short: Winslow Santa Fe Credit Union is a small NCUA-insured credit union based in Winslow, Arizona, offering savings accounts, CDs, mortgages, and loans.
description_long:
Winslow Santa Fe Credit Union was established in 1955 and operates as a state-chartered, NCUA-insured financial institution headquartered in Winslow, Arizona. With approximately $15.19 million in assets, $13.26 million in deposits, and $10.41 million in loans outstanding, it serves as a community-focused credit union for the Winslow area and surrounding regions. The institution maintains a capital base of $1.93 million and operates with a traditional credit union structure designed to serve its membership base.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of deposit and loan products. On the deposit side, they provide savings accounts and certificates of deposit (CDs) with various term lengths. For borrowing needs, Winslow Santa Fe offers mortgages (both 15-year and 30-year fixed options), home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), auto loans, and personal loans. The institution also provides interest-bearing checking accounts and other standard banking services typical of community credit unions.

As a smaller credit union, Winslow Santa Fe distinguishes itself through its local presence and community focus. The institution has been serving the Winslow area for nearly 70 years, suggesting established relationships with local members and deep community roots. Their NCUA insurance status provides the same deposit protection as larger banks, making them a safe option for consumers seeking local banking with federal backing.

Potential members should note that current rate information on the BestCashCow website appears incomplete, with deposit and loan rates not yet fully listed as of April 2026. This means prospective customers will need to contact the credit union directly for current rate quotes. The institution's small asset size means it may have more limited product offerings or features compared to larger regional or national credit unions, though it remains a legitimate and federally-insured banking option. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with multiple term options
pros: NCUA insured, providing same deposit protection as larger banks up to $250,000 per account
70+ years of history serving the Winslow, Arizona community since 1955
Offers competitive rates on CDs and mortgages as of the 2026 data shown (1-Year CD at 4.59%, 30-Year Mortgage at 4.91%)
Provides full-service banking including savings, CDs, mortgages, HELOCs, auto loans, and personal loans
State-chartered credit union with established local branch presence in Winslow
Member-focused credit union structure typically offering benefits to member-owners
Offers both short and long-term mortgage options (15-year and 30-year fixed)
cons: Very small institution with only $15.19 million in assets, limiting product selection and technological capabilities compared to larger credit unions
Current rate and product information appears incomplete on major financial websites, requiring direct contact for quotes
Limited geographic reach as a small community credit union based in Winslow, Arizona may restrict membership or services
Smaller institutions typically have fewer branch locations and ATM networks than regional credit unions
Limited online presence and digital banking information available compared to larger financial institutions
best_for: Winslow, Arizona residents seeking local community banking with federal insurance protection
Borrowers in the Winslow area looking for mortgages or home equity financing from an established local lender
Consumers preferring to bank with a member-owned credit union with deep community roots rather than corporate banks
diagnosis: Winslow Santa Fe Credit Union is best suited for residents of Winslow, Arizona who value local banking relationships and community-focused financial services with federal NCUA protection. The primary caveat is that as a very small credit union ($15.19M in assets), it has significantly limited product breadth, technology capabilities, and geographic reach compared to regional or national credit unions, and interested consumers must contact them directly for current rates since online information is incomplete.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
